commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=0f1c28fdf24ffb56b21f65ada069757f549fe80b)
commands.review_summary:
The PR lets media-understanding image, MiniMax fallback, and audio/video execution pass an empty API key for resolved `aws-sdk` auth and adds mocked Bedrock media regressions.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level reproduction is high confidence: current main documents Bedrock `aws-sdk` as keyless but the media image and audio/video paths still call static-key guards before provider execution. I did not run a live Bedrock call in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Human PR handling is needed because missing real Bedrock proof is a merge gate and the narrow retry fix should be reviewed in the provider-auth context.

target.title: fix(media): allow aws-sdk auth mode for image and audio/video paths
target.body:
The chat path's `prepareSimpleCompletionModel` exposes an
`allowMissingApiKeyModes` escape hatch so providers whose
`auth.mode === "aws-sdk"` (notably `amazon-bedrock`) can
resolve credentials at call time via the AWS SDK chain instead
of a static `apiKey`. The image and audio/video runtime paths
in `media-understanding` did not have the same escape: each
one unconditionally called `requireApiKey`, which throws on an
empty key, so the `image`/`audio`/`video` tools failed for
Bedrock deployments using BYOK with role/SSO/profile
credentials.

This patches the three sites in `media-understanding` that
resolve a primary api key (line refs updated to current
`HEAD`):

- `resolveImageRuntime` (`src/media-understanding/image.ts:139`,
  apiKey assignment at lines 240-249)
- `resolveMinimaxVlmFallbackRuntime`
  (`src/media-understanding/image.ts:413`, apiKey assignment
  at lines 430-431)
- `resolveProviderExecutionAuth`
  (`src/media-understanding/runner.entries.ts:421`,
  `allowEmptyExecution` boolean + `primaryApiKey` at lines
  450-451)

Each site follows the same shape: when the resolved auth key
is empty *and* the auth mode is `aws-sdk`, the api key is
passed through as `""` (the SDK resolves credentials at call
time); otherwise `requireApiKey` runs and the existing throw
semantics are preserved for every other auth mode. The image
runtime also skips `setRuntimeApiKey` for the empty-key
aws-sdk case so we don't write empty-string secrets into
runtime auth storage.

A second commit closes the next layer: `dedupeApiKeys` strips
the empty-string sentinel before `executeWithApiKeyRotation`,
so I added an opt-in `allowEmptyKey` option on the rotation
helper (single empty-key invocation when the deduped list
is empty) and threaded an `allowEmptyExecution` flag from
`resolveProviderExecutionAuth` through the audio and video
call sites.

Tests:

- `image.test.ts` â€” added regression covering the aws-sdk
  amazon-bedrock image describe path: `requireApiKey` is not
  called, `setRuntimeApiKey` is not called, and the underlying
  `complete()` is invoked with `apiKey: ""` so the AWS SDK
  auth chain takes over. 25/25 pass locally.
- `runner.aws-sdk.test.ts` â€” new file covering the audio
  (`transcribeAudio`) and video (`describeVideo`) paths
  end-to-end via `runCapability`. Each test asserts the
  empty-key sentinel reaches the provider call without
  `requireApiKey` being invoked.

Closes #72031.

### AI-assisted

- [x] Lightly tested. New tests cover the image, audio, and
  video describe paths on `amazon-bedrock` + `aws-sdk`.
  Targeted media-understanding files run clean: `image.test.ts`
  25/25 (verified locally on rebased HEAD), plus
  `runner.aws-sdk.test.ts` (verified on the original commit
  before the local rolldown crash on rerun).
- [x] I understand the change. The chat path landed an
  `allowMissingApiKeyModes` parameter when `aws-sdk` auth was
  first added (so the AWS SDK chain can resolve creds itself).
  The three media-understanding sites resolve auth via a
  shared helper but never carry the missing-key allowance
  through to `requireApiKey`. The fix is the narrowest
  equivalent: bypass `requireApiKey` when the resolved key is
  empty *and* the mode is `aws-sdk`, leave the throw in place
  for every other case.
- Did not run against a live Bedrock instance â€” no Bedrock
  credentials available in my environment. The fix mirrors the
  chat path that's already shipping for the same
  `auth.mode === "aws-sdk"` case, so the live-environment
  signal already exists in production via the chat path. A
  maintainer with Bedrock access running `openclaw` against
  `amazon-bedrock` for image/audio/video tools after this
  patch would close the loop.
target.author: truffle-dev

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
